Using `select()` without specifying columns fetches all columns from the USERS table, which can lead to maintenance issues if the table schema changes. Explicitly list the required columns, e.g., `context.select(USERS.ID, USERS.NAME, ...).from(USERS)`.

Using `select()` without specifying columns fetches all columns from the table, which can retrieve unnecessary data and cause performance issues. Specify only the required columns explicitly, e.g., `context.select(TABLE.COL1, TABLE.COL2).from(table).fetch()`.
